question.text: What is the concept of 'Beneficial Owner' in the context of AML/CFT guidelines?

answer.text: The natural person who ultimately owns or controls a client or on whose behalf a transaction is being conducted
The person whose name appears on the account opening form as the primary holder
The legal entity that holds the majority stake in a company
The nominee appointed to receive benefits in case of the account holder's death

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Beneficial Owner is the natural person who ultimately owns or controls a customer/entity, or the person on whose behalf a transaction is conducted. Identifying the beneficial owner is crucial to prevent criminals from hiding behind corporate structures or nominees. **The Trap:** The account holder's name may differ from the beneficial owner — shell companies and trusts are commonly used to obscure beneficial ownership. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) and NISM Series VI (Depository Operations) test beneficial ownership identification. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always look through the ownership chain to identify the natural person who controls 25% or more of the shares/capital — this is your beneficial owner threshold.
